message:
feat(web): replace composition-as-paste hack with native IME event pipeline

Rewrite the web crate's composition event handling to emit proper
Event::Ime variants instead of the previous workaround that converted
only "end"-phase composition events into synthetic unbracketted
PasteEvents and silently dropped all intermediate phases (start,
update, cancel).

input_parser.rs changes:
  - parse_composition_event() now maps all four composition phases
    (start, update, end/commit, cancel) to their corresponding
    ImeEvent constructors. Unknown phases produce an
    InputParseError::UnknownPhase error instead of being silently
    ignored.
  - The doc comment no longer claims composition events are dropped
    for non-end phases.
  - Tests rewritten: composition_end_produces_commit_ime_event (was
    composition_end_produces_paste), composition_update now returns
    an ImeEvent::update instead of None, empty-end produces an
    empty-text commit instead of None, and new tests for start and
    cancel phases added.

session_record.rs changes:
  - event_to_json() serializes Event::Ime with kind "ime", phase
    string, and text payload.
  - ime_phase_to_str() maps ImePhase variants to stable JSON strings.
  - parse_event_json() handles both "ime" and "composition" kind
    strings for backward compatibility with existing trace files.
    The "data" field alias is accepted alongside "text" for compat
    with frankenterm-format traces.
  - parse_ime_phase() maps phase strings including "end" as an alias
    for "commit".
  - New test: ime_event_record_replay exercises a full start -> update
    -> commit lifecycle through the recorder and verifies replay
    produces matching checksums.
  - Updated alias test covers composition "update" and "end" parsing
    through the frankenterm compat path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
